signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Risk + Energy Inflation
signals.text: JPY COT net short -105.2k (28% long) creates short-squeeze risk (USD/JPY downside if shorts cover) as WTI crude surged 11% on Iran war escalation. While the 264bp OIS carry advantage favors USD, the 162.35 close is vulnerable to a safe-haven JPY bid if the Hormuz risk premium triggers a broader equity liquidation. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 Jul is the catalyst for determining if energy-driven inflation forces a hawkish shift.

signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: NZD 1W performance (+1.320%) vs CHF laggard status (-0.458%) establishes a 1.778% G10 spread favoring the cross. The RBNZ 2.5% hike bias contrasts with the SNB 0.0% hold, providing a +250bp carry advantage that offsets the NZD COT net short of -29.5k. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 90% hike priced) on 2 Sep remains the primary anchor for this yield-seeking momentum.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Tariff Escalation + COT Crowding
signals.text: CAD COT net short -93.5k (20.6% long) faces further downside pressure following Trump's weekend threat of new Canadian tariffs. This protectionist shift reinforces the 132bp OIS carry advantage for USD/CAD, potentially driving the pair above the 1.4011 Friday close. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) is the catalyst for assessing if trade risks override the recent 1.10% CAD weekly outperformance.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hold Bias + Data Underperformance
signals.text: EUR COT net short -74.6k aligns with a strong negative CESI (-17 net misses), suggesting sustained downward pressure toward 1.14. The 144bp OIS carry advantage for USD is reinforced by the ECB's expected hold at the next meeting on 23 Jul. This meeting is the catalyst for confirming the divergence between the Fed's restrictive +1.47% real rate and the ECB's +0.03%.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Data Misses vs Carry Advantage
signals.text: AUD COT net long +21.9k (63.2% long) is at risk of an unwind as the AUD CESI shows a strong negative -13 net miss. While the -73bp OIS carry favors AUD, the 0.6970 Friday close faces headwinds from the Iran-driven risk-off sentiment and falling T-note yields. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 Aug determines if the 4.35% hold stance can withstand deteriorating domestic data.
